title: Child Development Support Coordinator (Healthcare)
description:
Job Highlights:

  • Must be able to travel to Jurong and Keat Hong

  • Up to $3,200

  • Monday – Friday, 8:30am – 6:00pm

  • Yearly Contract Renewable

  • AWS, VB, Yearly Increment

We are seeking an Assistant Coordinator to support a multidisciplinary healthcare team in both administrative and clinical coordination functions. This role is ideal for someone organised, service-oriented, and comfortable working in a structured healthcare environment supporting children and families.

Key Responsibilities

Appointment & Scheduling Coordination

  • Manage and open appointment slots for clinicians

  • Schedule, reschedule, and coordinate patient appointments (including teleconsults)

  • Maintain patient waitlists and track clinicians’ schedules and room utilisation

  • Liaise with schools and external partners for appointment arrangements

Programme & Therapy Support

  • Assist in coordinating group programmes, talks, and caregiver training sessions

  • Prepare letters, forms, and materials for programmes

  • Support room setup and ensure equipment is cleaned and maintained

  • Register participants and manage required documentation

Case Coordination Support

  • Support care coordination for patients with complex needs

  • Liaise with families, clinicians, and community partners

  • Ensure patients follow clinical pathways and appointments are carried out in sequence

  • Provide basic patient education when required

Data & Administrative Support

  • Assist in clinical data collection and reporting

  • Extract and organise statistics and reports from internal systems

  • Manage documentation, filing, and inventory tracking

  • Support basic data entry and system updates

  • Assist therapists with setup of therapy equipment and sessions when required

Requirements

  • Diploma in any discipline

  • Minimum 1 year of relevant experience in administrative, coordination, or healthcare-related experience preferred

  • Good communication and interpersonal skills

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office

  • Organised, detail-oriented, and able to multitask

  • Positive attitude with the ability to work independently and in a team

  • Service-oriented and comfortable working with children and families

Applicants who possess relevant experience for the above responsibilities are most welcome to apply. If you do not possess the above experience, your application will still be considered on individual merits and you may be contacted for other opportunities.

Please submit your updated resume in MS Words format by using the APPLY NOW BUTTON or email the updated resume to miya@mci.com.sg

By submitting your personal data and/or resume, you give consent to collection, use and disclosure of your personal data and/or resume by the company (or its agent) for the purpose of the processing and administration by the company relating to this job application.
